assertion_results.text: Description includes a Summary section with a 1-3 sentence overview mentioning OAuth2 or Google login
assertion_results.evidence: First line states 'This PR adds Google OAuth2 login to the application'

assertion_results.text: Description includes a Motivation section explaining WHY (user feedback, 60% requested social login)
assertion_results.evidence: No motivation section. Description jumps directly from summary to file list. No mention of user feedback or the 60% statistic.

assertion_results.text: Description includes a Changes section that groups changes by concept (auth flow, data model, configuration) rather than listing files one by one
assertion_results.evidence: Changes listed as a file-by-file list (8 bullet points, one per file) with no conceptual grouping

assertion_results.text: Description includes a Testing Instructions section with numbered, copy-pasteable steps for verifying the OAuth flow
assertion_results.evidence: Testing section says 'Run the tests with npm test. You can also test manually by setting up Google OAuth credentials and trying the login flow.' — not numbered, not copy-pasteable, no specific steps

assertion_results.text: Description includes a Rollback Plan section that addresses the database migration (ALTER TABLE) and how to reverse it
assertion_results.evidence: No rollback plan section present in the description

assertion_results.text: Description includes a Reviewer Notes section that flags security-sensitive areas (token handling, OAuth callback, session management)
assertion_results.evidence: Notes section says 'This is a new feature that adds social login. The existing email/password authentication is unchanged.' — no security guidance for reviewers

assertion_results.text: Testing instructions include at least one edge case or error scenario (e.g., user denies consent, invalid token, duplicate Google account)
assertion_results.evidence: No edge cases mentioned. Testing section only says 'Run the tests' and 'try the login flow'

assertion_results.text: Rollback plan mentions the database migration specifically and whether it is reversible
